title: centaurea nigra subsp. debeauxii
intro: Centaurea nigra subsp. debeauxii, also known as De Beaux's knapweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the sunflower family native to Europe. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving menstrual cramps, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the flowers and leaves of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
